tax: Placed at genus rank, Claymaw is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a water-swelling clay body that smothers. A new species belongs here only if it shows a swelling-clay body fed by a permanent seep.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a low slumping mound with a folding lip. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a radial-cracked drying pan — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Claymaw occupies wet clay galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a seep keeps a bentonite bed animated and recur with the seep refills after drought, sustaining themselves on osmotic swelling pressure.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: rears a fold and collapses it over prey. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: swelling mass and moisture-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: engulfing and hard-setting. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Claymaw matters because makes wet galleries a smothering trap. It lets survey reports sort wet smother from dry abrasion without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Claymaw is the genus-rank grouping of wet swelling-clay elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a bentonite body that swells on wetting to engulf, and divide below into species groups by clay chemistry and seep strength. It doubles its size on contact with water.
